description_html:
This is a competitive, hands-on event where participants design a custom 8-bit instruction 
system using Verilog (hardware) and Assembly (software). Performance is evaluated using 
an external hardware counter provided by the organizers, which measures execution speed 
for a predefined workload. 
Participants are encouraged to explore micro-architectural optimizations such as pipelining, 
instruction scheduling, parallelism, and execution efficiency within strict design constraints. 

Objectives:

  • Test real hardware–software co-design skills 
  • Encourage micro-architecture innovation under constraints 
    Evaluate performance optimization, not just correctness 
  • Expose participants to low-level system thinking (ISA ↔ datapath ↔ control ↔ software) 
  • Simulate industry-style performance benchmarking 
  • Design your own ISA 

Rules and Regulations:

  • Architecture Rules 
    • Custom ISA allowed, but must be documented 
    • Only one core allowed 
    • Clock frequency must be fixed (defined by organizers) 
    • External performance counter must not be modified or bypassed 
    • All designs must be fully synthesizable 
  • Software Rules 
    • Programs must be written only in assembly 
    • No hard-coded results or lookup tables 
    • Code must execute only on the designed CPU 
  • Submission Rules 
  • Submit: 
    • Verilog source files 
    • Assembly program 
    • ISA documentation 
    • Micro-architecture explanation (1–2 pages) 
  • Late submissions → disqualified 

Note:

  • In case of any disputes, final decision will taken by the organisers. People are expected to bring their own extension boxes.

Expected Tools to Be Installed:

  • Mandatory 
  • Verilog HDL simulator (any one): 
  • ModelSim / Questa 
  • Icarus Verilog 
  • Verilator 
  • Vivado 
  • Recommended 
  • GTKWave (waveform analysis) 
  • Python / Bash (automation & testing) 
  • Make / shell scripting tools 

Disallowed Actions:

  • Modifying or tampering with the external counter 
  • Using behavioral shortcuts (e.g., pre-computed outputs) 
  • Using any language other than recommended. 
  • Hard-coding answers for benchmark tasks 
  • Clock manipulation or overclocking 
  • Using multi-cycle tricks that violate ISA semantics 
  • Copying designs from open-source CPUs without attribution 
  • Any violation = instant disqualification. No warnings. 

Evaluation Criteria:

  • Execution speed (counter value) 
  • Correctness of output 
  • Architectural innovation 
  • ISA & design documentation 
  • Code clarity & structure 
  • Weight 
    • 40% 
    • 25% 
    • 15% 
    • 10% 
    • 10% 
  • Tie-breaker: Lower cycle count wins. 

Event Duration:

  • Total duration: 4–5 hours 
  • Architecture design & coding: 2–4 hours 
  • Testing & optimization: 1–2 hours 
  • Final evaluation & demo: 1 hour 
  • (Optional: multi-day version for advanced participants) 

Target Audience:

  • Undergraduate & postgraduate students in: 
    • Electronics & Communication 
    • Electrical Engineering 
    • Computer Engineering 
    • Embedded Systems 
  • Strong fit for: 
    • Computer Architecture enthusiasts 
    • VLSI & RTL design learners 
    • Low-level systems programmers
location: Indian Institute of Information Technology Design and Manufacturing
